verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It is synthesized with a signal peptide, processed through the ER/Golgi, and secreted into the extracellular milieu — primarily seminal plasma and prostatic fluid. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented stable covalent or wash-resistant tethering to a cell-surface TM partner. It is not a lysosomal TM protein. Its surface story is entirely secreted-soluble: it cleaves pro-PSA extracellularly but is not stably anchored to the PM. No documented cancer ecto-pool on the outer leaflet, no cell-death-induced PM translocation, no germline-restricted surface display, no activation-induced PM pool, and no PM cycling reported. Therapeutic programs (e.g., antibody-drug conjugates) against KLK2 target the secreted or shed protein, not a cell-surface-anchored form. All five contextual buckets are ruled out by absence of any membrane-anchoring mechanism.
